channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: War in the Middle East hits humanitarian aid supply chains
item.description: The global supply chain for humanitarian aid, disrupted by the conflict in the Middle East, may not return to normal until 2027, the United Nations warns.

item.title: Widespread condemnation of Russia's actions after drone crash in Romania
EU ministers to discuss Ebola outbreak in DRC in special video conference
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) has declared a public health emergency of international concern, with cross-border risks requiring international coordination.

item.title: Belgium 'mostly silent' about Iran's protester executions, says dissident
item.description: "As with other European countries, Belgium has been totally sidelined. Its silence is the policy of appeasement,” says the exiled Ali Bagheri.

item.title: Gaza's journalists to receive international award
MSF workers undergo crash-course before deployment to Ebola-hit area
item.description: Some 25 Medecins Sans Frontieres staffers are following a two-day crash course on medical practices and protocols related to Ebola before heading to the DRC.

item.title: Norway is joining France's nuclear deterrent system, Macron says
item.description: Norway has decided to join France’s 'advanced nuclear deterrence' initiative with European allies, President Macron announced on Wednesday.

item.title: Queen Sonja of Norway hospitalised with heart problems
item.description: Queen Sonja of Norway has been hospitalised in Oslo due to heart-related issues, the royal palace announced on Wednesday.

item.title: Oil prices rise after US resumes attacks in Iran
item.description: The US bombed Iranian missile launch platforms and vessels on Monday, despite an ongoing ceasefire and peace negotiations.

item.title: Intense fighting reported in eastern DRC as Ebola rages on
item.description: Intense fighting reported between government forces and rebels in the east of the Democratic Republic of Congo, even as an Ebola outbreak rages in the area.

item.title: Pope calls for AI to be 'disarmed' to 'prevent it from dominating humanity'
item.description: In his first encyclical, entitled "Magnifica Humanitas", the American pope argues that AI cannot be regarded as morally neutral.

item.title: Hajj pilgrimage begins amid extreme temperatures
item.description: Authorities in Saudi Arabia have installed powerful air-conditioning systems around the grounds of the Grand Mosque.

item.title: US says a 'solid' agreement with Iran may be reached on Monday
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the agreement could enable Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: United States 'optimistic' about agreement with Iran
item.description: For the first time since February, an LNG tanker navigates Hormuz. Could US-Iran agreement bring an end to the blockade?

item.title: Russian bombing claims four lives in Kyiv
item.description: Four killed, including children, as Russia launches missiles and drones at Kyiv in one of the deadliest attacks yet. Ukraine intercepts over 600 aerial threats overnight.

item.title: Princess Elisabeth to graduate from Harvard University
item.description: Crown Princess Elisabeth’s Harvard graduation marks the end of a seven-year global academic pursuit. Questions remain about what’s next in her royal path.

item.title: Ebola death toll reaches 200 in DRC
item.description: Armed conflict and remote locations hinder containment as Ebola death toll rises in the DRC. Global health officials issue warnings over the Bundibugyo strain's fatal impact.

item.title: Suspect died after shooting at White House
item.description: Saturday's fatal shooting near the White House raises questions after reports link the suspect to prior encounters with security agencies and psychiatric admission.

item.title: Iran said to be 'finalising' memorandum with United States
item.description: Iran and the U.S. edge closer to a 14-point agreement to end the war, yet critical points like the Strait of Hormuz blockade remain divisive. Will progress hold?

item.title: Captain of ship contacting hantavirus finally leaves deck
item.description: As the hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship claims three lives, hundreds of international contacts are under surveillance, with some yet to be traced. WHO calls journey "extraordinary and frightening."
